title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: GLMAEDr R package running General Lake Model (GLM) coupled Aquatic EcoDynamics (AED) library. Rather bundling pre-compiled executables (like GLM3r), GLMAEDr compiles GLM source machine — avoiding code-signing restrictions macOS producing binary tuned system.

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: Install calling glm_install(). check whether build dependencies present:

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: Install package GitHub pak:

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: Run installing package. clones GLM-AED source repository compiles binary (~2 minutes): binary stored per-user data directory (tools::R_user_dir("GLMAEDr", "data")), survives package upgrades requires administrator privileges. Confirm installation succeeded:
code: library(GLMAEDr) glm_install() glm_is_installed()   # TRUE glm_version()        # prints the GLM version string

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: simulation lives folder must contain glm3.nml configuration file. Copy bundled template starting point: Edit glm3.nml configure lake geometry, meteorological inputs, output settings.

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: GLM writes output my_lake/output/output.nc default. Verbose output printed console; pass verbose = FALSE suppress .

title: GLMAEDr — Run GLM-AED from R
text: need v4 development branch access private AED_Tools repository, use glm_install_aed_tools() instead:
code: # Public AED_Tools repo, v4alpha branch glm_install_aed_tools(ref = "v4alpha")  # Private AED_Tools_Private repo (requires GitHub credentials) glm_install_aed_tools(private = TRUE, ref = "v4alpha")

what: Check that all tools required to build GLM are available — check_build_tools
title: Check that all tools required to build GLM are available — check_build_tools
text: Verifies git, Fortran compiler (gfortran), C compiler (gcc/cc), make, NetCDF development headers present. Aborts platform-specific installation instructions anything missing.

title: Check that all tools required to build GLM are available — check_build_tools
Check that all tools required to build GLM are available — check_build_tools
text: Invisibly TRUE tools found.

what: Path to the installed GLM executable — glm_exe_path
title: Path to the installed GLM executable — glm_exe_path
text: Returns expected path GLM binary per-user data directory. file may exist yet glm_install() run.

title: Path to the installed GLM executable — glm_exe_path
Path to the installed GLM executable — glm_exe_path
text: Character string.

what: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
text: Downloads GLM-AED source repository compiles GLM executable current platform. resulting binary stored per-user data directory survives package upgrades requires administrator privileges. needs run per machine (GLM version upgrade).

title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
code: glm_install(   repo = glm_default_repo,   ref = "HEAD",   no_gui = TRUE,   jobs = 1L,   force = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
text: repo URL GLM-AED-style git repository. Forks mirrors supported long preserve glm-source/build_glm.sh structure. ref check cloning. Accepts: "HEAD" (default) – tip default branch branch name, e.g. "develop" tag, e.g. "v3.9.107" full abbreviated commit SHA, e.g. "a3f2c1d" no_gui Logical. Build headless version GLM (graphical output window). Recommended use R. Default TRUE. jobs Integer. Number parallel make jobs. Defaults 1L. Fortran module compilations implicit ordering dependencies can race parallel make; increase verified upstream Makefiles declare explicit module dependencies. force Logical. Reinstall even binary already exists. quiet Logical. Suppress build output. Default FALSE.

title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
text: Invisibly returns path installed GLM executable.

title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install
text: macOS – install via Homebrew:   Linux (Ubuntu/Debian) – install via apt:   Windows – install RTools R version (provides gfortran, make, NetCDF libraries).
code: brew install gcc netcdf sudo apt-get install gfortran libnetcdf-dev debhelper

title: Install the GLM executable by compiling from source — glm_install

what: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
text: Clones AED_Tools repository (private counterpart), fetches GLM source via fetch_sources.sh, optionally switches GLM sub-repository specific branch tag, compiles GLM build_glm.sh. resulting binary stored per-user data directory glm_install().

title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
code: glm_install_aed_tools(   repo = aed_tools_default_repo,   ref = "HEAD",   private = FALSE,   no_gui = TRUE,   jobs = 1L,   force = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
text: repo URL AED_Tools git repository. Ignored private = TRUE. ref Branch tag check inside GLM/ sub-repository fetching sources. Accepts: "HEAD" (default) – whatever branch fetch_sources.sh leaves checked branch name, e.g. "v4alpha" tag, e.g. "v4.0.0" full abbreviated commit SHA, e.g. "a3f2c1d" private Logical. Clone AED_Tools_Private instead public AED_Tools repository. Requires git credentials (SSH key personal access token) read access github.com/AquaticEcoDynamics/AED_Tools_Private. Default FALSE. no_gui Logical. Build headless version GLM. Default TRUE. jobs Integer. Number parallel make jobs. Default 1L. force Logical. Reinstall even binary already exists. quiet Logical. Suppress build output. Default FALSE.

title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
text: Invisibly returns path installed GLM executable.

title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools
text: glm_install() – see ?glm_install details.

title: Install GLM from the AED_Tools repository — glm_install_aed_tools

title: Check whether GLM is installed — glm_is_installed

Check whether GLM is installed — glm_is_installed

Full path to the installed GLM executable — glm_path

what: Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary
title: Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary
text: Copies existing GLM executable per-user data directory run_glm() package functions can find . Use obtained GLM binary means (e.g. downloaded release page, received colleague, built manually outside R) want compile source.

title: Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary
Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary
text: path Path GLM executable register. Must existing file. POSIX systems file made executable copying. force Logical. Overwrite existing installation. Default FALSE.

title: Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary
text: Invisibly returns path installed GLM executable.

title: Register a pre-downloaded GLM binary — glm_register_binary

title: Return the GLM version string — glm_version

Return the GLM version string — glm_version

title: Return the GLM version string — glm_version

title: Path to the bundled NML template — nml_template_path
text: Returns absolute path example glm3.nml template shipped package. Copy file simulation directory starting point new configurations.

title: Path to the bundled NML template — nml_template_path
Run a GLM simulation — run_glm
text: Runs installed GLM-AED executable simulation stored sim_folder. folder must contain valid NML configuration file. GLM yet installed, error instructions raised.

title: Run a GLM simulation — run_glm
code: run_glm(   sim_folder = ".",   nml_file = "glm3.nml",   verbose = TRUE,   args = character() )

title: Run a GLM simulation — run_glm
text: sim_folder Path directory containing simulation files. Defaults current working directory. nml_file Name NML configuration file inside sim_folder. Defaults "glm3.nml". verbose Logical. Whether echo GLM stdout/stderr console. Defaults TRUE. args Character vector additional command-line arguments pass GLM executable.

title: Run a GLM simulation — run_glm
text: Invisibly returns processx::run() result list elements status, stdout, stderr.

title: Run a GLM simulation — run_glm
